> merge, sort, update, error check, etc... or is there a lot more?

Quite a bit, yes.


> 2. I'm using Mainframe Express at school, and Merant at home. I dislike
> both. Is there a reasonabley priced Cobol compiler that I can get?
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
> programs and excercises.

Very good.   You will need to "get" tables.  Once you do, they will be easy.  I
don't know where to get the exercises - did you read our FAQ?


> 4. I will never post asking for someone to do my homework for me. But if I
> ever encounter problems is it okay to post asking for general help and
> direction?

Certainly, especially with your attitude.  Read our FAQ.   When you post, tell
us your environment (OS and compiler), show us what you did, what results you
got, and what results you expected.  Let us know what you tried and make sure
you give us sufficient code.   Some people will tell the answer, but most will
try to guide you to getting the answer.
```

And mine.

Great.  I love COBOL, and I'm always happy to see young 
people taking it up. 

>This is my second course.
>Please bear with me. So far, we've covered many things. Headers, error
…[11 more quoted lines elided]…

The most important omissions that I see on the programming
side are Database interface and On-line screen interface.  

>2. I'm using Mainframe Express at school, and Merant at home. 
>I dislike
>both. Is there a reasonably priced Cobol compiler that I can get?
>
I use Microfocus and Fujitsu COBOL.  Both are fine.  
As for price, well...it's been so long since I've used any
software that isn't pirated, I have no idea.  
Fujitsu was giving theirs away free when I got it.  I don't know
if they are charging now.  

>3. I need extensive help with tables. I really want to learn this. It is not
>my goal just to pass. I have straight A's. I find passing very very easy.
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>programs and excercises.

I think tables--and their Parent, the whole concept of Looping--are
what computer programming is all about.  
By all means, master Tables and all aspects of tables--defining, 
indexing, searching, etc.  

>
>4. I will never post asking for someone to do my homework for me. But >if I
>ever encounter problems is it okay to post asking for general help and
>direction?
>
For homework problems, be sure to include the code that
represents your failed attempts.  Many people will be happy
to correct your errors, but few will want to do your assignment
from scratch.  


Getting back to Question 1, in the business environment you
will spend an ungodly amount of time reading, writing, talking,
and listening.  Be sure to include these topics in your
curriculum.  Far more systems fail because of poor 
communication than because of poor code.  



Ross
http://www.geocities.com/ross_klatte/

For Homework!

I'm working on this assignment. Supposed to update a master indexed file.
I wrote the program and everything. Once I run it though, I get the
following message:
"File information missing for indexed file". I got my test data from a
student disk
that is provided with our text. I am assuming that there should be a file
along with
the indexed master with a .idx extension maybe? The book does not say how
to create the actual indexed file from scratch.

My source is available at http://falcon.tp.devry.edu/~oabhari/cbl/Lab15.txt
Master file available at http://falcon.tp.devry.edu/~oabhari/cbl/M1501.txt
Transaction file available at
http://falcon.tp.devry.edu/~oabhari/cbl/T1501.txt

Restating the question: Am I missing a file that gives info about the
indexed master? and if
yes, how can I recreate the master so that I'd have that missing file?

thanks a whole bunch.

Omar
```

#### ↳ Re: Indexed Files!

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2001-05-05T21:20:34-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9d2ce4$n11$1@slb7.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<9F0J6.121806$rX5.6538588@news1.rdc1.il.home.com>`

```
In your source code you have:

           SELECT CUSTOMER-MASTER-IO
               ASSIGN TO 'C:\CBL\LAB15\DATA\M1501.NDX'
               ORGANIZATION IS INDEXED
               ACCESS IS RANDOM
               RECORD KEY IS MAST-CUST-NO.
                           ....
         OPEN ...
                I-O   CUSTOMER-MASTER-IO.

This assumes that the file 'C:\CBL\LAB15\DATA\M1501.NDX' already exists and
is an "indexed file".

What you need (as your question implied) is a way to "make" your existing
master file (that ends in ".txt" - and which APPEARS to be a "sequential"
file into an indexed file.

There are a couple of ways to do this (assuming that you class didn't
actually "give" you an indexed file to copy to your PC - that you haven't
seen yet).

1) Write a "quick and dirty" program that simply reads the existing
sequential (.txt) file and writes out each record to a new indexed (.ndx)
file.

2) If you have (as I think you do) one of the MERANT products that includes
"WFL" - use it to create an "indexed file" from a "sequential file" (this is
a vendor supplied utility - that I think is still part of Mainframe
Express - but may or may not be a part of Personal COBOL)

P.S.  Good coding "technique" - especially for indexed files
   *ALWAYS* check your file status codes after every I/O statement
(including OPEN, WRITE, READ, etc).  If it is not zero (or at least the 1st
byte as '0') the chances are that "something" is wrong.  This may be a
problem with your program logic OR it may be a "real world problem" that you
need to provide code in your program to handle.
```

#### ↳ Re: Indexed Files!

- **From:** John H Sleight Jr <nospam@newsranger.com>
- **Date:** 2001-05-07T17:06:45+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<FCAJ6.4671$vg1.365612@www.newsranger.com>`
- **References:** `<9F0J6.121806$rX5.6538588@news1.rdc1.il.home.com>`

```
Hi Omar,

I noticed that in your post you used ".idx" and in your pgm you used ".ndx".
Could this be your problem?
If not Bill's suggestion is probably the way to go. I worked with another
student and she whipped up a COBOL pgm to create the indexed file from a flat
file.

Jack

### Help: sequential read of an indexed file defined Access is random

- **From:** goble@gtech (David. E. Goble)
- **Date:** 2001-05-06T13:49:17+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3af5540f.5502373@news.adelaide.on.net>`

```
Hi all;

I have an indexed file of;

           SELECT f400-pbhcap-file
               ASSIGN TO DISK "data/pbhcap.dat"
               ORGANIZATION IS INDEXED
               ACCESS IS RANDOM
               RECORD KEY IS f410-indexed-key
               FILE STATUS IS w120-file-status.

       01 f400-pbhcap-rec.
           05 f410-indexed-key.
               10 f409-match                   Pic 9(02).
               10 f408-mname.
                   15 f408a-fname              Pic x(15).
                   15 f408a-lname              Pic x(15).
           05 f420-score1                      Pic 9(03).
           05 f430-score2                      Pic 9(03).
           05 f440-score3                      Pic 9(03).
           05 f450-pbest                       Pic 9(03).
           05 f460-hcap                        Pic 9(03).
           05 f470-grade                       Pic x.

Given either a f409-match or a f408-mname and the file has been sorted
with;
           SORT f600-sgrades-file
               ON ASCENDING KEY f609-match
                                f608-mname
               USING f400-pbhcap-file
               GIVING f400-pbhcap-file.

How can I create a single control break report, based on the match?
```

:>I have an indexed file of;

:>           SELECT f400-pbhcap-file

:>           05 f420-score1                      Pic 9(03).

:>           05 f440-score3                      Pic 9(03).

:>                                f608-mname
:>               USING f400-pbhcap-file

sequentially with ACCESS IS RANDOM?

The same way you would do it with a sequential file.

                                     value "Any more members?
[Yy/Nn]:".
           05 match-selection                  Pic 9(02).
           05 member-selection                 Pic 9(03).
           05 cal-selection                    Pic 9(02).
           05 selector                         Pic x.
               88 sup                           value"U", "u", "<",
",".
               88 sdown                        value "D", "d", ">",
".".
               88 sselect                                value "S",
"s".
               88 squick                                 value "Q",
"q".
               88 sdetailed                              value "D",
"d".
               88 sreport                                value "R",
"r".
               88 ssend                                  value "E",
"e".
               88 sgradings                              value "G",
"g".
               88 sscores                                value "S",
"s".
               88 scancel                                value "C",
"c".
           05 add-new-member                   Pic x(30)
                                                value "Add New
Members".
           05 all-members                      Pic x(30)
                                                    value "All
Members".
           05 instructions.
               10 i1                           Pic x(80)
                       value "To move up press [U/u/,/<], To move
down".
               10 i2                           Pic x(80)
           value "press [D/d/./>], To select current value press
[S/s]".
               10 i3                           Pic x(80)
                                     value "press [Q/q, D/d, R/r or
E]".
               10 i4                           Pic x(80)
             value "press [Tab or arrow keys to move thru entry
fields".
               10 i5                           Pic x(21)
                                          value " has a max score of:
".
            05 calibras                        Pic x(03).
               88 cal177                                    value
"177".
               88 cal22S                                    value
"22S".
               88 cals22                                    value
"22s".
               88 cal22L                                    value
"22L".
               88 call22                                    value
"22l".
               88 cal22                                     value
"22".
               88 cal32                                     value
"32".
               88 cal38                                     value
"38".
               88 cal357                                    value
"357".
               88 cal9mm                                    value
"9mm".
               88 cal44                                     value
"44".
               88 cal45                                     value
"45".
       01 w700-matches-table.
           05 w705-match-sub                   Pic 9(02).
           05 w710-by-match OCCURS 16 TIMES INDEXED BY w710-match-inx.
               10 w711-match                   Pic x(29).
               10 w711a-stages                 Pic 9(01).
               10 w711d-out-of                 Pic 9(03).
               10 w712-by-grade OCCURS 4 TIMES
                                              INDEXED BY
w912-grade-inx.
                   15 w712b-grade              Pic 9(03).
               10 w713-match-calibra OCCURS 10 TIMES
                                                INDEXED BY
w713-cal-inx.
                   15 w713a-cal                Pic 9.
                   15 w713b-cal-type           Pic x(03).
               10 w714-per-stage OCCURS 4 TIMES
                                                INDEXED BY
w714-per-inx.
                   15 w714a-stage-max          Pic 9(03).
       01 w800-line.
           05 filler                           Pic x(40)
                       value
"----------------------------------------".
           05 filler                           Pic x(40)
                       value
"----------------------------------------".
       01 w900-msg.
           05 w990-display-message             Pic x(70).
           05 w980-display-action              Pic x(80).
       01 w1000-gradings.
           05 w1100-heading1.
               10 w1110-date                   Pic x(10).
               10 filler                       Pic x(15) value spaces.
               10 filler                       Pic x(27)
                                    value "Kangaroo Island Pistol
Club".
               10 filler                       Pic x(19) value spaces.
               10 filler                       Pic x(06) value "Page:
".
               10 w1120-page                   Pic 9(03).
           05 w1200-heading2.
               10 filler                       Pic x(33) value spaces.
               10 filler                       Pic x(14)
                                                 value "Match
Gradings".
               10 filler                       Pic x(33) value spaces.
           05 w1400-control1.
               10 w1410-match-name             Pic x(29).
               10 filler                       Pic x(51) value spaces.
           05 w1500-detailheading.
               10 w1510-detailheading1.
                   15 filler                   Pic x(22) value spaces.
                   15 filler                   Pic x(04) value "Name".
                   15 filler                   Pic x(16) value spaces.
                   15 filler                   Pic x(10)
                                                     value "last
three".
                   15 filler                   Pic x(11) value spaces.
                   15 filler                   Pic x(08)
                                                       value
"Personal".
                   15 filler                   Pic x(20) value spaces.
               10 w1520-detailheading2.
                   15 filler                   Pic x(13) value spaces.
                   15 filler                   Pic x(05) value
"First".
                   15 filler                   Pic x(09) value spaces.
                   15 filler                   Pic x(07)
                                                        value
"Surname".
                   15 filler                   Pic x(09) value spaces.
                   15 filler                   Pic x(06) value
"Scores".
                   15 filler                   Pic x(05) value spaces.
                   15 filler                   Pic x(08)
                                                       value
"handicap".
                   15 filler                   Pic x(02) value spaces.
                   15 filler                   Pic x(04) value "Best".
                   15 filler                   Pic x(02) value spaces.
                   15 filler                   Pic x(05) value
"Grade".
                   15 filler                   Pic x(05) value spaces.
           05 w1600-detail-line.
               10 filler                       Pic x(09) value spaces.
               10 w1610-fname                  Pic x(15).
               10 filler                       Pic x value spaces.
               10 w1620-lname                  Pic x(15).
               10 filler                       Pic x(02) value spaces.
               10 w1630-score1                 Pic 9(03).
               10 filler                       Pic x value spaces.
               10 w1640-score2                 Pic 9(03).
               10 filler                       Pic x value spaces.
               10 w1650-score3                 Pic 9(03).
               10 filler                       Pic x(05) value spaces.
               10 w1660-hcap                   Pic 9(03).
               10 filler                       Pic x(05) value spaces.
               10 w1670-pbest                  Pic 9(03).
               10 filler                       Pic x(05) value spaces.
               10 w1680-grade                  Pic x.
               10 filler                       Pic x(06) value spaces.
       SCREEN SECTION.
      *---------------
       01 ss100-Main-screen.
           05 BLANK SCREEN.
           05 LINE  1 COLUMN 01 value "Kangaroo Island Pistol Club".
           05         COLUMN 64 value "DATE: ".
           05         COLUMN 70 Pic x(10) from w220-Date.
       01 ss200-Member-Selection.
           05 LINE  3 COLUMN 16
                            value
"-----------------------------------".
           05 LINE  4 COLUMN 02 value "Members Name: |".
           05         COLUMN 18 Pic x(15) FROM w511-mname-first.
           05         COLUMN 34 PIC x(15) FROM w512-mname-last.
           05         COLUMN 50 value "|".
           05 LINE  5 COLUMN 16
                            value
"-----------------------------------".
           05                   Pic x TO selector auto.
       01 ss210-member-detail.
           05 LINE  3 COLUMN 01 value "Members first name: ".
           05         COLUMN 21 Pic x(15) USING w511-mname-first.
           05         COLUMN 37 value "last name: ".
           05         COLUMN 48 Pic x(15) USING w512-mname-last.
           05 LINE  5 COLUMN 01 value "Postal address".
           05 LINE  6 COLUMN 04 value "Street #:".
           05         COLUMN 14 Pic 9(04) USING w551-pstreetno.
           05         COLUMN 20 value "Street:".
           05         COLUMN 28 Pic x(15) USING w552-pstreet.
           05 LINE  8 COLUMN 04 value "Sub/town:".
           05         COLUMN 14 Pic x(15) USING w553-psub-town.
           05         COLUMN 32 value "postcode:".
           05         COLUMN 42 Pic 9(04) USING w554-ppcode.
           05 LINE 10 COLUMN 01 value "Home phone".
           05         COLUMN 20 value "Work phone".
           05 LINE 11 COLUMN 06 value "(".
           05         COLUMN 07 Pic 9(03) USING w521-phharea.
           05         COLUMN 10 value ")".
           05         COLUMN 12 Pic 9(04) USING w522-phhfirst4.
           05         COLUMN 17 Pic 9(04) USING w523-phhsecond4.
           05         COLUMN 26 value "(".
           05         COLUMN 27 Pic 9(03) USING w521-phwarea.
           05         COLUMN 30 value ")".
           05         COLUMN 32 Pic 9(04) USING w522-phwfirst4.
           05         COLUMN 37 Pic 9(04) USING w523-phwsecond4.
           05 LINE 13 COLUMN 01 value "Occupation:".
           05         COLUMN 13 Pic x(30) USING w540-occupation.
           05 LINE 15 COLUMN 01
                        value "Does this member have a pistol
licence?".
           05         COLUMN 41 Pic x TO w140-press-a-key.
           05 LINE 17 COLUMN 01 value "Licence Renewal".
           05 LINE 18 COLUMN 14 value "Day   Month".
           05 LINE 19 COLUMN 15 Pic 9(02) USING w562-lr-day.
           05         COLUMN 18 value "/".
           05         COLUMN 20 Pic 9(02) USING w561-lr-month.
       01 ss300-Match-Selection.
           05 LINE  7 COLUMN 16
                              value
"---------------------------------".
           05 LINE  8 COLUMN 04 value "Match Type: |".
           05         COLUMN 18 Pic x(33) FROM

w711-match(match-selection).
           05         COLUMN 48 value "|".
           05 LINE  9 COLUMN 16
                              value
"---------------------------------".
           05                   Pic x TO selector auto.
       01 ss400-Button-Selection.
           05 LINE 12 COLUMN 08 value "Score".
           05 LINE 13 COLUMN 02 value "Q uick".
           05         COLUMN 10 value "D etailed".
           05         COLUMN 25 value "R eport".
           05         COLUMN 43 value "E nd program".
           05                   Pic x TO selector auto.
       01 ss500-score-sheet.
           05 ss510-score-match.
               10 LINE  3 COLUMN 16 Pic x(33) FROM

w711-match(match-selection).
               10         COLUMN 54 value "Date:".
               10         COLUMN 60 Pic 9(02) USING f313c-day.
               10         COLUMN 62 value "/".
               10         COLUMN 63 Pic 9(02) USING f313b-month.
               10         COLUMN 65 value "/".
               10         COLUMN 66 Pic 9(04) USING f313a-year.
           05 ss520-score-member.
               10 LINE  5 COLUMN 01 value "Name:".
               10         COLUMN 07 Pic x(15) FROM w511-mname-first.
               10         COLUMN 21 PIC x(15) FROM w512-mname-last.
               10         COLUMN 35 value "Cal:".
               10         COLUMN 40 Pic x(03) FROM
                      w713b-cal-type(match-selection, cal-selection).
               10         COLUMN 43 Pic x TO selector auto.
           05 ss530-quick-score.
               10 LINE  9 COLUMN 29 value "Aggregate:".
               10         COLUMN 40 Pic 9(03) USING f390-aggregate.
           05 ss540-detail-score.
               10         COLUMN 01 value "Stage:".
               10         COLUMN 08 Pic 9 FROM w330-stage.
               10         COLUMN 10 Pic 9(03) TO w340-stage-val.
       01 ss600-Report-Selection.
           05 LINE 12 COLUMN 08 value "Reports".
           05 LINE 13 COLUMN 02 value "G radings".
           05         COLUMN 14 value "S cores".
           05         COLUMN 43 value "C ancel reporting".
           05                   Pic x TO selector auto.
       01 ss900-msg-screen.
           05 LINE 22 COLUMN 01 value "MESSAGE: ".
           05 LINE 23 COLUMN 10 Pic x(70) FROM w990-display-message.
           05 LINE 24 COLUMN 01 Pic x(80) FROM w980-display-action.
           05 LINE 24 COLUMN 65 Pic x TO w140-press-a-key.
       PROCEDURE DIVISION.
      ********************
       0000000-MAIN-MODULE.
      *====================
           PERFORM 1000000-INITIALISE-MODULE.
           PERFORM 2000000-PROCESS-MODULE UNTIL w111-eof.
           PERFORM 3000000-END-MODULE.
           STOP RUN.
       1000000-INITIALISE-MODULE.
      *==========================
           PERFORM 1300000-SET-DATE.
           PERFORM 1400000-INITIALISE-VARS.
       1300000-SET-DATE.
      *=================
           MOVE FUNCTION CURRENT-DATE TO w210-system-date.
           STRING  w213-day "/"
                   w212-month "/"
                   w211-year
                   DELIMITED BY SIZE
                   INTO w220-Date
           END-STRING.
       1400000-INITIALISE-VARS.
      *========================
          MOVE ZEROS TO w130-Menu-selection
                        w320-temp-hcap.
          MOVE SPACES TO w990-display-message
                         w980-display-action.
          MOVE SPACES TO  w140-press-a-key.
          MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file.
          MOVE 16 TO match-selection.
          INITIALIZE w500-members-rec.
          MOVE ZEROS TO member-selection.
          MOVE add-new-member TO w510-members-name.
          OPEN INPUT   f100-matches-file
               I-O     f200-members-file
               I-O     f300-scores-file
               I-O     f400-pbhcap-file
               INPUT   f500-calibras-file.
           PERFORM 141000-LOAD-MATCH-ARRAY
               VARYING w710-match-inx FROM 1 BY 1
               UNTIL w710-match-inx>15 OR w111-eof.
           PERFORM 141110-LOAD-CALIBRA-ARRAY
               VARYING w713-cal-inx FROM 1 BY 1
               UNTIL w713-cal-inx>9 OR w111-eof.
          MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file.
          PERFORM 142000-ADD-TO-ARRAY.
          MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file.
          PERFORM 143000-READ-MEMBER-HEADER.
          MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file.
       141000-LOAD-MATCH-ARRAY.
      *========================
           READ f100-matches-file
               AT END
                   MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file
               NOT AT END
                   PERFORM 141100-LOAD-MATCH
           END-READ.
       141100-LOAD-MATCH.
      *==================
           MOVE f110-matches-match TO w711-match(w710-match-inx).
           MOVE f180-matches-max TO w711d-out-of(w710-match-inx).
           MOVE f120-grades-gradem TO w712b-grade(w710-match-inx, 1).
           MOVE f130-grades-gradea TO w712b-grade(w710-match-inx, 2).
           MOVE f140-grades-gradeb TO w712b-grade(w710-match-inx, 3).
           MOVE f150-grades-gradec TO w712b-grade(w710-match-inx, 4).
           MOVE f160-match-cal177 TO w713a-cal(w710-match-inx, 1).
           MOVE f160-match-cal22s TO w713a-cal(w710-match-inx, 2).
           MOVE f160-match-cal22l TO w713a-cal(w710-match-inx, 3).
           MOVE f160-match-cal32 TO w713a-cal(w710-match-inx, 4).
           MOVE f160-match-cal38 TO w713a-cal(w710-match-inx, 5).
           MOVE f160-match-cal357 TO w713a-cal(w710-match-inx, 6).
           MOVE f160-match-cal9mm TO w713a-cal(w710-match-inx, 7).
           MOVE f160-match-cal44 TO w713a-cal(w710-match-inx, 8).
           MOVE f160-match-cal45 TO w713a-cal(w710-match-inx, 9).
           MOVE f170-match-stages TO w711a-stages(w710-match-inx).
           MOVE f171-mstage1-max TO w714a-stage-max(w710-match-inx,
1).
           MOVE f172-mstage2-max TO w714a-stage-max(w710-match-inx,
2).
           MOVE f173-mstage3-max TO w714a-stage-max(w710-match-inx,
3).
           MOVE f174-mstage4-max TO w714a-stage-max(w710-match-inx,
4).
           MOVE "PRA" TO w713b-cal-type(w710-match-inx, 10).
           MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file.
       141110-LOAD-CALIBRA-ARRAY.
      *==========================
           READ f500-calibras-file
               AT END
                   MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file
               NOT AT END
                   PERFORM 141111-LOAD-CALIBRA
                       VARYING w710-match-inx FROM 1 BY 1
                           UNTIL w710-match-inx>15
                   MOVE "PRA" TO
                            w713b-cal-type(w710-match-inx,
w713-cal-inx)
           END-READ.
       141111-LOAD-CALIBRA.
      *====================
           MOVE f510-calibra TO
                           w713b-cal-type(w710-match-inx,
w713-cal-inx).
       142000-ADD-TO-ARRAY.
      *====================
           MOVE "All Matches" TO w711-match(w710-match-inx).
       143000-READ-MEMBER-HEADER.
      *==========================
           DISPLAY ss100-Main-screen.
           MOVE 1 TO w150-Rel-Key.
           READ f200-members-file INTO w400-members-rec
               INVALID KEY
                   MOVE "There are no members, create members [Yy/Nn]"
                                                  TO
w980-display-action
                   PERFORM 2999999-DISPLAY-MESG
                   IF(NOT w111-eof)
                   THEN
      * get user to enter member details... etc...
      * then update/create header file...
                       MOVE 3 TO w410-first-key
                                 w420-lst-key
                       PERFORM 143100-GET-MEMBER-DETAILS UNTIL
w111-eof
                   ELSE
                       MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file
                   END-IF
               NOT INVALID KEY
                   MOVE w420-lst-key TO w150-Rel-key
           END-READ.
       143100-GET-MEMBER-DETAILS.
      *==========================
           DISPLAY ss100-Main-screen.
           MOVE SPACES TO w510-members-name.
           PERFORM 143110-VALIDATE-MEMBER-DETAILS UNTIL w111-eof.
           DISPLAY ss210-member-detail.
           MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file.
           MOVE a-anymore TO w980-display-action.
           MOVE SPACES TO w990-Display-message.
           PERFORM 2999999-DISPLAY-MESG.
           MOVE w140-press-a-key TO w110-end-of-file.
           IF(NOT w111-eof)
           THEN
               IF(w150-Rel-key>=w420-lst-key)
               THEN
                   ADD 1 TO w420-lst-key
               END-IF
               PERFORM 143120-WRITE-MEMBER-HEADER
               MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file
           END-IF.
           PERFORM 143130-WRITE-MEMBER-REC.
       143110-VALIDATE-MEMBER-DETAILS.
      *===============================
           DISPLAY ss210-member-detail.
           MOVE i4 TO w990-display-message.
           DISPLAY ss900-msg-screen.
           ACCEPT ss210-member-detail.
           MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file.
           PERFORM 143111-VALID-POSTCODE.
           PERFORM 143112-WORK-PHONE-SAME-AS-HOME.
           IF(w140-press-a-key EQUALS "Y" OR
                                            w140-press-a-key EQUALS
"y")
           THEN
               PERFORM 143113-MEMBER-HAS-LICENCE
           ELSE
               MOVE ZEROS TO w560-lic-renewal
           END-IF.
       143111-VALID-POSTCODE.
      *======================
           IF(w554-ppcode EQUALS ZEROS)
           THEN
               MOVE "error invalid postcode" TO w990-display-message
               PERFORM 2900000-ERROR
               MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file
           END-IF.
       143112-WORK-PHONE-SAME-AS-HOME.
      *===============================
           IF(w520-phhome EQUALS ZEROS)
           THEN
               MOVE "error must have a home phone number"
                                                 TO
w990-display-message
               PERFORM 2900000-ERROR
               MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file
           ELSE
               IF(w520-phwork EQUALS ZEROS)
               THEN
                   MOVE w520-phhome TO w520-phwork
               END-IF
           END-IF.
       143113-MEMBER-HAS-LICENCE.
      *==========================
           IF(w562-lr-day EQUALS ZEROS OR w562-lr-day>31)
           THEN
               MOVE "error invalid renewal day" TO
w990-display-message
                   PERFORM 2900000-ERROR
                   MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file
               ELSE
                   IF(w561-lr-month EQUALS ZEROS OR w561-lr-month>12)
                   THEN
                       MOVE "error invalid renewal month"
                                                 TO
w990-display-message
                       PERFORM 2900000-ERROR
                       MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file
                   END-IF
           END-IF.
       143120-WRITE-MEMBER-HEADER.
      *===========================
           MOVE 1 to w150-Rel-Key
           WRITE f200-members-rec FROM w400-members-rec
               INVALID KEY
                   IF(w120-file-status EQUALS 22)
                   THEN
                       PERFORM 143121-REWRITE-MEMBER-HEADER
                   ELSE
                       MOVE "error writing member header  "
                                                  TO
w980-display-action
                       PERFORM 2888888-FILE-STATUS
                       PERFORM 2900000-ERROR
                   END-IF
           END-WRITE.
       143121-REWRITE-MEMBER-HEADER.
      *=============================
           REWRITE f200-members-rec FROM w400-members-rec
               INVALID KEY
                   MOVE "error rewriting member header  "
                                                  TO
w980-display-action
                   PERFORM 2888888-FILE-STATUS
                   PERFORM 2900000-ERROR
           END-REWRITE.
       143130-WRITE-MEMBER-REC.
      *========================
           MOVE w420-lst-key TO w150-Rel-Key
                                member-selection.
           WRITE f200-members-rec FROM w500-members-rec
               INVALID KEY
                   MOVE "error writing member details  "
                                                  TO
w980-display-action
                   PERFORM 2888888-FILE-STATUS
                   PERFORM 2900000-ERROR
           END-WRITE.
       2000000-PROCESS-MODULE.
      *=======================
           DISPLAY ss100-Main-screen.
           PERFORM 2100000-MEMBERS UNTIL w111-eof.
           MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file.
           PERFORM 2200000-MATCH-SELECTION UNTIL w111-eof.
           MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file.
      * buttons... quick score/detailed score/report/quit-exit/cancel
           DISPLAY ss400-Button-Selection.
           MOVE SPACES TO w990-Display-message.
           MOVE i3 TO w980-display-action.
           DISPLAY ss900-msg-screen.
           MOVE SPACES TO w990-display-message
                          w980-display-action.
           ACCEPT ss400-Button-Selection.
           DISPLAY ss100-Main-screen.
           PERFORM 2300000-DO-BUTTON-SELECTION.
       2100000-MEMBERS.
      *================
           PERFORM 2110000-MEMBER-SELECTION UNTIL w111-eof.
           IF(member-selection EQUALS ZEROS)
           THEN
               MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file
               PERFORM 143000-READ-MEMBER-HEADER
               PERFORM 143100-GET-MEMBER-DETAILS UNTIL w111-eof
               MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file
               DISPLAY ss100-Main-screen
           END-IF.
           PERFORM 2110000-MEMBER-SELECTION UNTIL w111-eof.
       2110000-MEMBER-SELECTION.
      *========================
           DISPLAY ss200-Member-Selection.
           MOVE i1 TO w990-display-message.
           MOVE i2 TO w980-display-action.
           DISPLAY ss900-msg-screen.
           MOVE SPACES TO w990-display-message
                          w980-display-action.
           ACCEPT ss200-Member-Selection.
           EVALUATE TRUE
               WHEN sup
                   PERFORM 2111000-MOVE-MEMBER-UP
               WHEN sdown
                   PERFORM 2112000-MOVE-MEMBER-DOWN
               WHEN sselect
                   MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file
           END-EVALUATE.
           MOVE SPACES TO selector.
           IF(member-selection>=w410-first-key)
           THEN
               MOVE member-selection TO w150-Rel-Key
               PERFORM 2113000-READ-MEMBER-REC
           END-IF.
       2111000-MOVE-MEMBER-UP.
      *=======================
           IF(member-selection>=w420-lst-key)
           THEN
               MOVE ZEROS TO member-selection
           ELSE
               ADD 1 TO member-selection
           END-IF.
           EVALUATE member-selection
               WHEN ZERO
                   MOVE add-new-member TO w510-members-name
               WHEN 1
                   ADD 1 TO member-selection
                   MOVE all-members TO w510-members-name
               WHEN 2
                   MOVE all-members TO w510-members-name
           END-EVALUATE.
       2112000-MOVE-MEMBER-DOWN.
      *=========================
           IF(member-selection<=ZEROS)
           THEN
               MOVE w420-lst-key TO member-selection
           ELSE
               SUBTRACT 1 FROM member-selection
           END-IF.
           EVALUATE member-selection
               WHEN ZERO
                   MOVE add-new-member TO w510-members-name
               WHEN 1
                   SUBTRACT 1 FROM member-selection
                   MOVE add-new-member TO w510-members-name
               WHEN 2
                   MOVE all-members TO w510-members-name
           END-EVALUATE.
       2113000-READ-MEMBER-REC.
      *========================
           READ f200-members-file INTO w500-members-rec
               INVALID KEY
                   MOVE "error reading members file  "
                                                  TO
w980-display-action
                   PERFORM 2888888-FILE-STATUS
                   PERFORM 2900000-ERROR
           END-READ.
       2200000-MATCH-SELECTION.
      *========================
           DISPLAY ss300-Match-Selection.
           MOVE i1 TO w990-display-message.
           MOVE i2 TO w980-display-action.
           DISPLAY ss900-msg-screen.
           MOVE SPACES TO w990-display-message
                          w980-display-action.
           ACCEPT ss300-Match-Selection.
           EVALUATE TRUE
               WHEN sup
                   PERFORM 2210000-MOVE-MATCH-UP
               WHEN sdown
                   PERFORM 2220000-MOVE-MATCH-DOWN
               WHEN sselect
                   MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file
           END-EVALUATE.
           MOVE SPACES TO selector.
       2210000-MOVE-MATCH-UP.
      *======================
           IF(match-selection=16)
           THEN
               MOVE 1 TO match-selection
           ELSE
               ADD 1 TO match-selection
           END-IF.
       2220000-MOVE-MATCH-DOWN.
      *========================
           IF(match-selection=1)
           THEN
               MOVE 16 TO match-selection
           ELSE
               SUBTRACT 1 FROM match-selection
           END-IF.
       2300000-DO-BUTTON-SELECTION.
      *============================
           EVALUATE TRUE
               WHEN squick
               WHEN sdetailed
                   IF(match-selection EQUALS 16)
                   THEN
                       PERFORM PERFORM 2310000-SCORE
                           VARYING match-selection FROM 1 BY 1
                               UNTIL match-selection>15 OR w111-eof
                   ELSE
                       PERFORM PERFORM 2310000-SCORE
                   END-IF
                   MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file
               WHEN sreport
                   PERFORM PERFORM 2320000-REPORT
                   MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file
               WHEN ssend
                   MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file
           END-EVALUATE.
       2310000-SCORE.
      *==============
           PERFORM 2311000-VALIDATE-MATCH-DATE UNTIL w111-eof.
           MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file.
           IF(member-selection>=w410-first-key)
           THEN
               MOVE member-selection TO w150-Rel-Key
               PERFORM 2312000-MEMBERS
           ELSE
               PERFORM 2312000-MEMBERS
                   VARYING w150-Rel-Key FROM w410-first-key BY 1
                       UNTIL w150-Rel-Key>w420-lst-key OR w111-eof
           END-IF.
           MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file.
       2311000-VALIDATE-MATCH-DATE.
      *============================
           MOVE w210-system-date  TO f313-match-date.
           DISPLAY ss510-score-match.
           ACCEPT ss510-score-match.
           MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file
           IF(f313c-day EQUALS ZEROS OR f313c-day>31)
           THEN
               MOVE "error invalid match day" TO w990-display-message
               PERFORM 2900000-ERROR
               MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file
           ELSE
               IF(f313b-month EQUALS ZEROS OR f313b-month>12)
               THEN
                   MOVE "error invalid match month"
                                                 TO
w990-display-message
                   PERFORM 2900000-ERROR
                   MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file
               ELSE
                   IF(f313a-year<1990 OR f313a-year>3000)
                   THEN
                       IF(f313a-year NOT ZEROS)
                       THEN
                           MOVE "error invalid match year [1990 -
3000]"
                                                 TO
w990-display-message
                           PERFORM 2900000-ERROR
                           MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file
                       ELSE
                           MOVE w211-year TO f313a-year
                           DISPLAY ss510-score-match
                   END-IF
               END-IF
           END-IF.
       2312000-MEMBERS.
      *================
           PERFORM 2113000-READ-MEMBER-REC.
           IF(NOT w111-eof)
           THEN
               MOVE selector TO w140-press-a-key
               MOVE 1 TO cal-selection
               PERFORM 2312500-CALIBRA-SELECTION UNTIL w111-eof
               MOVE w140-press-a-key TO selector
               MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file
               EVALUATE TRUE
                   WHEN squick
                       PERFORM 2312100-QUICK-SCORE
                   WHEN sdetailed
                       PERFORM 2312200-DETAILED-SCORE
               END-EVALUATE
           END-IF.
           MOVE SPACES TO w990-display-message
                          w980-display-action.
           PERFORM 2312300-READ-PBHCAP-FILE.
           MOVE f460-hcap TO f395-hcap-attime
           PERFORM 2312400-WRITE-SCORE.
       2312100-QUICK-SCORE.
      *====================
           MOVE ZEROS TO f390-aggregate.
           PERFORM 2312110-VALIDATE-SCORE UNTIL w111-eof.
           MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file
           MOVE match-selection TO f312-match-shot
                                   f409-match.
           MOVE w510-members-name  TO f311-shooters-name
                                      f408-mname.
           MOVE ZEROS TO f350-stage1
                         f360-stage2
                         f370-stage3
                         f380-stage4.
       2312110-VALIDATE-SCORE.
      *=======================
           DISPLAY ss530-quick-score.
           STRING w711-match(match-selection)
               DELIMITED BY SIZE
                  i5
               DELIMITED BY SIZE
                  w711d-out-of(match-selection)
               DELIMITED BY SIZE
                   INTO w990-display-message
           END-STRING.
           DISPLAY ss900-msg-screen.
           ACCEPT ss530-quick-score.
           MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file.
           IF(f390-aggregate>w711d-out-of(match-selection))
           THEN
               MOVE "error aggregate is too high"
                                                 TO
w990-display-message
               PERFORM 2900000-ERROR
               MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file
           END-IF.
       2312200-DETAILED-SCORE.
      *=======================
           MOVE 4 TO w350-line-count.
           DISPLAY SPACES AT LINE w350-line-count.
           MOVE ZEROS TO f390-aggregate.
           PERFORM 2312210-GET-STAGE-VALUES
               VARYING w330-stage FROM 1 BY 1
                   UNTIL w330-stage>w711a-stages(match-selection).
           DISPLAY ss530-quick-score.
           MOVE match-selection TO f312-match-shot
                                   f409-match.
           MOVE w510-members-name TO f311-shooters-name
                                     f408-mname.
       2312210-GET-STAGE-VALUES.
      *=========================
           PERFORM 2312211-VALIDATE-STAGE UNTIL w111-eof.
           MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file.
           ADD 2 TO w350-line-count.
       2312211-VALIDATE-STAGE.
      *=======================
           DISPLAY ss540-detail-score AT LINE w350-line-count.
           STRING w711-match(match-selection)
               DELIMITED BY SIZE
                  i5
               DELIMITED BY SIZE
                  w714a-stage-max(match-selection, w330-stage)
               DELIMITED BY SIZE
                   INTO w990-display-message
           END-STRING.
           DISPLAY ss900-msg-screen.
           ACCEPT ss540-detail-score AT LINE w350-line-count.
           MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file.
           PERFORM 2312211a-VALID-STAGE.
       2312211a-VALID-STAGE.
      *=====================
           IF(w340-stage-val<=
                           w714a-stage-max(match-selection,
w330-stage))
           THEN
               EVALUATE w330-stage
                   WHEN 1
                       MOVE w340-stage-val TO f350-stage1
                   WHEN 2
                       MOVE w340-stage-val TO f360-stage2
                   WHEN 3
                       MOVE w340-stage-val TO f370-stage3
                   WHEN 4
                       MOVE w340-stage-val TO f380-stage4
               END-EVALUATE
               ADD w340-stage-val TO f390-aggregate
           ELSE
               MOVE "error stage is too high" TO w990-display-message
               PERFORM 2900000-ERROR
               MOVE "n" TO w110-end-of-file
           END-IF.
       2312300-READ-PBHCAP-FILE.
      *=========================
           READ f400-pbhcap-file
               INVALID KEY
                   IF(w120-file-status EQUALS 23)
                   THEN
                       MOVE ZEROS TO f450-pbest
                                     f440-score3
                                     f430-score2
                                     f420-score1
                       MOVE "D" TO f470-grade
                       MOVE w711d-out-of(match-selection) TO f460-hcap
                       WRITE f400-pbhcap-rec
                           INVALID KEY
                               MOVE "error writing pbhcap  "
                                                  TO
w980-display-action
                               PERFORM 2888888-FILE-STATUS
                               PERFORM 2900000-ERROR
                       END-WRITE
                   ELSE
                       MOVE "error reading pbhcap  "
                                                  TO
w980-display-action
                       PERFORM 2888888-FILE-STATUS
                       PERFORM 2900000-ERROR
                   END-IF
           END-READ.
       2312400-WRITE-SCORE.
      *====================
           WRITE f300-scores-rec
               INVALID KEY
                   MOVE "error writing score file  "
                                                  TO
w980-display-action
                   PERFORM 2888888-FILE-STATUS
                   PERFORM 2900000-ERROR
               NOT INVALID KEY
                   MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file
                   PERFORM 2312410-PBHCAP
           END-WRITE.
       2312410-PBHCAP.
      *===============
           PERFORM 2312300-READ-PBHCAP-FILE.
           IF(f340-calibra NOT EQUAL "PRA")
           THEN
               IF(f390-aggregate>f450-pbest)
               THEN
                   MOVE f390-aggregate TO f450-pbest
                   PERFORM 2312410a-SET-GRADE
               END-IF
               PERFORM 2312411-CAL-HCAP
               PERFORM 2312412-REWRITE-PBHCAP-FILE
           END-IF.
       2312410a-SET-GRADE.
      *===================
           IF(f390-aggregate<w712b-grade(match-selection, 1))
           THEN
               MOVE "A" TO f470-grade
               IF(f390-aggregate<w712b-grade(match-selection, 2))
               THEN
                   MOVE "B" TO f470-grade
                   IF(f390-aggregate<w712b-grade(match-selection, 3))
                   THEN
                       MOVE "C" TO f470-grade
                       IF

(f390-aggregate<w712b-grade(match-selection,4))
                       THEN
                           MOVE "D" TO f470-grade
                       END-IF
                   END-IF
               END-IF
           ELSE
               MOVE "M" TO f470-grade
           END-IF.
       2312411-CAL-HCAP.
      *=================
           MOVE f430-score2 TO f420-score1.
           MOVE f440-score3 TO f430-score2.
           IF(f430-score2>ZERO AND f420-score1>ZERO)
           THEN
               ADD f390-aggregate
                   f420-score1
                   f430-score2 GIVING w320-temp-hcap
               DIVIDE w320-temp-hcap BY 3 GIVING w320-temp-hcap
               SUBTRACT w320-temp-hcap
                   FROM w711d-out-of(match-selection)
               GIVING w320-temp-hcap
           ELSE
               MOVE w711d-out-of(match-selection) TO w320-temp-hcap
           END-IF.
           MOVE f390-aggregate TO f440-score3.
           MOVE f460-hcap TO f395-hcap-attime
           IF(w320-temp-hcap<f460-hcap)
           THEN
               MOVE w320-temp-hcap TO f460-hcap
           END-IF.
       2312412-REWRITE-PBHCAP-FILE.
      *============================
           REWRITE f400-pbhcap-rec
               INVALID KEY
                   MOVE "error rewriting pbhcap  "
                                                  TO
w980-display-action
                   PERFORM 2888888-FILE-STATUS
                   PERFORM 2900000-ERROR
           END-REWRITE.
       2312500-CALIBRA-SELECTION.
      *==========================
           DISPLAY ss520-score-member.
           MOVE i1 TO w990-display-message.
           MOVE i2 TO w980-display-action.
           DISPLAY ss900-msg-screen.
           MOVE SPACES TO w990-display-message
                          w980-display-action.
           ACCEPT ss520-score-member.
           EVALUATE TRUE
               WHEN sup
                   PERFORM 2210000-MOVE-CALIBRA-UP
               WHEN sdown
                   PERFORM 2220000-MOVE-CALIBRA-DOWN
               WHEN sselect
                   PERFORM 2312510-VALID-CALIBRA
           END-EVALUATE.
           MOVE SPACES TO selector.
       2312510-VALID-CALIBRA.
      *======================
           IF(cal-selection<10)
           THEN
               IF(w713a-cal(match-selection, cal-selection)>ZEROS)
               THEN
                   MOVE w713b-cal-type(match-selection, cal-selection)
                                                        TO
f340-calibra
               ELSE
                   MOVE w713b-cal-type(match-selection, 10)
                                                         TO
f340-calibra
               END-IF
           ELSE
               MOVE w713b-cal-type(match-selection, 10) TO
f340-calibra
           END-IF.
           MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file.
       2210000-MOVE-CALIBRA-UP.
      *========================
           IF(cal-selection>10)
           THEN
               MOVE 1 TO cal-selection
           ELSE
               ADD 1 TO cal-selection
           END-IF.
       2220000-MOVE-CALIBRA-DOWN.
      *========================
           IF(cal-selection<1)
           THEN
               MOVE 10 TO cal-selection
           ELSE
               SUBTRACT 1 FROM cal-selection
           END-IF.
       2320000-REPORT.
      *===============
           DISPLAY ss200-Member-Selection.
           DISPLAY ss300-Match-Selection.
           DISPLAY ss600-Report-Selection.
           ACCEPT ss600-Report-Selection.
           EVALUATE TRUE
               WHEN sscores
                   CLOSE f300-scores-file
                   PERFORM 2321000-REPORT-SCORES
                   OPEN I-O     f300-scores-file
               WHEN sgradings
                   CLOSE f400-pbhcap-file
                   PERFORM 2322000-REPORT-PBHCAP
                   OPEN I-O     f400-pbhcap-file
               WHEN scancel
                   MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file
           END-EVALUATE.
       2321000-REPORT-SCORES.
      *======================
           SORT f700-sscores-file
               ON DESCENDING KEY f712-match-shot
                                 f713-match-date
               ON ASCENDING KEY f730-aggregate
                                f780-hcap-attime
               USING f300-scores-file
               GIVING f300-scores-file.
      *
      *    IF(match-selection EQUALS 16)
      *    THEN
      *        do all matches
      *    ELSE
      *        do a match
      *    END-IF.
      *
       2322000-REPORT-PBHCAP.
      *======================
           PERFORM 2322100-INITIAL-REPORT.
           PERFORM 2322200-REPORT-HEADINGS.
           PERFORM 2322310-REPORT-CONTROL-BREAK.
           PERFORM 2322300-REPORT-DETAILS
           CLOSE f400-pbhcap-file
                 f900-report-file.
       2322100-INITIAL-REPORT.
      *=======================
           SORT f600-sgrades-file
               ON ASCENDING KEY f609-match
                                f608-mname
               USING f400-pbhcap-file
               GIVING f400-pbhcap-file.
           OPEN I-O    f400-pbhcap-file
                OUTPUT f900-report-file.
           MOVE ZEROS TO w1120-page
                         w350-line-count.
           PERFORM 2322110-MEMBER-KEY-INITIAL-SET.
           PERFORM 2322120-MATCH-KEY-INITIAL-SET.
           PERFORM 2322130-READ-PBHCAP-FILE.
       2322110-MEMBER-KEY-INITIAL-SET.
      *===============================
           IF(member-selection>=w410-first-key)
           THEN
               MOVE member-selection TO w150-Rel-Key
           ELSE
               MOVE w410-first-key TO w150-Rel-Key
               PERFORM 2113000-READ-MEMBER-REC
               MOVE w510-members-name TO f408-mname
           END-IF.
       2322120-MATCH-KEY-INITIAL-SET.
      *==============================
           IF(match-selection EQUALS 16)
           THEN
               MOVE 1 TO f409-match
           ELSE
               MOVE match-selection TO f409-match
           END-IF.
       2322130-READ-PBHCAP-FILE.
      *=========================
           READ f400-pbhcap-file
               INVALID KEY
                   MOVE "y" TO w110-end-of-file
               NOT INVALID KEY
                   MOVE f409-match TO w360-old-match
           END-READ.
       2322200-REPORT-HEADINGS.
      *========================
           ADD 1 TO w1120-page.
           MOVE w220-Date TO w1110-date.
           WRITE f900-report-rec FROM w1100-heading1
               AFTER ADVANCING PAGE.
           WRITE f900-report-rec FROM w1200-heading2
               AFTER ADVANCING 2 LINES.
           MOVE 2 TO w350-line-count.
       2322300-REPORT-DETAILS.
      *=======================
           IF(f409-match NOT EQUAL TO w360-old-match)
           THEN
               PERFORM 2322310-REPORT-CONTROL-BREAK
           END-IF.
           PERFORM 2322320-SET-UP-REPORT.
           PERFORM 2322330-PRINT-REPORT.
           PERFORM 2322340-SET-MEMBER-KEY-PART.
           IF(match-selection EQUALS 16 AND f409-match NOT >15)
           THEN
               ADD 1 TO f409-match
           END-IF.
           PERFORM 2322130-READ-PBHCAP-FILE.
       2322310-REPORT-CONTROL-BREAK.
      *=============================
           MOVE w711-match(f409-match) TO w1410-match-name.
           WRITE f900-report-rec FROM w1400-control1
               AFTER ADVANCING 2 LINES.
           WRITE f900-report-rec FROM w1510-detailheading1
           AFTER ADVANCING 2 LINES.
           WRITE f900-report-rec FROM w1520-detailheading2.
           MOVE f409-match TO w360-old-match.
           MOVE SPACES TO w1410-match-name.
           ADD 3 TO w350-line-count.
       2322320-SET-UP-REPORT.
      *======================
           MOVE f408a-fname TO w1610-fname.
           MOVE f408a-lname TO w1620-lname.
           MOVE f420-score1 TO w1630-score1.
           MOVE f430-score2 TO w1640-score2.
           MOVE f440-score3 TO w1650-score3.
           MOVE f450-pbest TO w1660-hcap.
           MOVE f460-hcap TO w1670-pbest.
           MOVE f470-grade TO w1680-grade.
       2322330-PRINT-REPORT.
      *=====================
           IF(w350-line-count>25)
           THEN
               PERFORM 2322200-REPORT-HEADINGS
           END-if.
           WRITE f900-report-rec FROM w1600-detail-line
               AFTER ADVANCING 2 LINES.
           ADD 1 TO w350-line-count.
       2322340-SET-MEMBER-KEY-PART.
      *============================
           ADD 1 TO w150-Rel-Key.
           PERFORM 2113000-READ-MEMBER-REC.
           MOVE w510-members-name TO f408-mname.

       2900000-ERROR.
      *==============
           MOVE FUNCTION LENGTH(a-to-continue) TO w311-lenA.
           MOVE FUNCTION LENGTH(w980-display-action) TO w312-lenB.
           COMPUTE w313-center=(w312-lenB - w311-lenA)/2.
           MOVE a-to-continue TO w980-display-action(w313-center:).
           PERFORM 2999999-DISPLAY-MESG.
       2888888-FILE-STATUS.
      *====================
           EVALUATE w120-file-status
               WHEN 22
                   STRING w980-display-action
                       DELIMITED BY "  "
                          " duplicate key"
                       DELIMITED BY SIZE
                           INTO w990-display-message
                   END-STRING
               WHEN OTHER
                   STRING w980-display-action
                       DELIMITED BY "  "
                          " "
                       DELIMITED BY SIZE
                          w120-file-status
                       DELIMITED BY SIZE
                           INTO w990-display-message
                   END-STRING
           END-EVALUATE.
           MOVE SPACES TO w980-display-action.
       2999999-DISPLAY-MESG.
      *=====================
           DISPLAY ss900-msg-screen.
           ACCEPT ss900-msg-screen.
           MOVE SPACES TO w990-display-message
                          w980-display-action.
       3000000-END-MODULE.
      *===================
           CLOSE   f100-matches-file
                   f200-members-file
                   f300-scores-file
                   f400-pbhcap-file.
#####################################
>--
>Binyamin Dissen <bdissen@dissensoftware.com>
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>Director, Dissen Software, Bar & Grill - Israel
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Help: sequential read of an indexed file defined Access is random

- **From:** Binyamin Dissen <postingid@dissensoftware.com>
- **Date:** 2001-05-07T12:08:07+03:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<poocft8k2dm92dpp8aenaamm0k6emnu06k@4ax.com>`
- **References:** `<3af5540f.5502373@news.adelaide.on.net> <4qraftcqr8k8ukfimmbsksq2e77u7e95h6@4ax.com> <3af5ea1a.3523623@news.adelaide.on.net>`

```
On Mon, 07 May 2001 00:24:42 GMT goble@gtech (David. E. Goble) wrote:

:>On Sun, 06 May 2001 18:38:27 +0300, Binyamin Dissen
:><postingid@dissensoftware.com> wrote:

:>>Show us the code.

What are your results and what do you expect the results to be?

I also notice that you are sorting an INDEXED file with USING/GIVING back to
the file. Perhaps INDEXED files are implemented on PCs, but I would expect
that an INDEXED file cannot be resorted and stored back in a different
sequence.

   [ snipped ]

:>           SELECT f300-scores-file
:>               ASSIGN TO DISK "data/scores.dat"
:>               ORGANIZATION IS INDEXED
:>               ACCESS IS RANDOM
:>               RECORD KEY IS f310-indexed-key
:>               FILE STATUS IS w120-file-status.
:>           SELECT f400-pbhcap-file
:>               ASSIGN TO DISK "data/pbhcap.dat"
:>               ORGANIZATION IS INDEXED
:>               ACCESS IS RANDOM
:>               RECORD KEY IS f410-indexed-key
:>               FILE STATUS IS w120-file-status.

   [ snipped ]

:>           SORT f700-sscores-file
:>               ON DESCENDING KEY f712-match-shot
:>                                 f713-match-date
:>               ON ASCENDING KEY f730-aggregate
:>                                f780-hcap-attime
:>               USING f300-scores-file
:>               GIVING f300-scores-file.

   [ snipped ]

:>           SORT f600-sgrades-file
:>               ON ASCENDING KEY f609-match
:>                                f608-mname
:>               USING f400-pbhcap-file
:>               GIVING f400-pbhcap-file.

   [ snipped ]
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Re: Help: sequential read of an indexed file defined Access is random

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** johann.schischa@OEAG.AT ("Schischa Johann")
- **Date:** 2001-05-08T08:40:06+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<01May8.103505cest.119045@gateway.oeag.at>`
- **References:** `<poocft8k2dm92dpp8aenaamm0k6emnu06k@4ax.com>`

That's right, if you would like to use an other key, define secundary key or create temporary file sorted the way you want.

I apologize in advance for being "off topic", but having negotiated numerous
labor agreements as a "labor official" I can't resist.

Over the period of one calendar year a person who works  40-hour weeks will
average 173.3 hours per month.

It's surpirsing how many so-called bean counters didn't correctly calculate
this into their cost projections.  It was fun to nail them.
```

<a id="t-86e15386d7"></a>
## Thread: Cutting accepted string — 1 this month / 7 total

_Topics: [`Language features and syntax`](topics.md#syntax)_

_Thread started [2001-04](2001-04.md#t-86e15386d7)._

#### ↳ Re: Cutting accepted string

- **From:** chammond@lc.cc.il.us
- **Date:** 2001-05-07T16:49:22+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9d6jmi$bh6$1@news.netmar.com>`
- **References:** `<grxF6.64$DR.4119@read2.inet.fi>`

```
In article <grxF6.64$DR.4119@read2.inet.fi>, Jarkko K�hk�nen
<jarkko.kahkonen@softageneraattori.fi> writes:
>Hiallo. I made a program which asks your name and prints string into screen.
>Please read and help.

Some people recommended using inspect with tallying.  I agree with them but I
would use function reverse so you can count the spaces on the end of the
field and not the spaces used.  However I dont know if your specific compiler
has this option or not.  The function Reverse may be compiler specific. 
Otherwise it is hard to tell where to stop counting.  Some people have 2 3 or
4 names.

COBOL also has some String commands that may be able to append the comma onto
the name field and make a new field with the name and the comma in it.  The
syntax for the string command is a little tricky but there may be an example
in your book. 

  Good luck.
>
>IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
…[32 more quoted lines elided]…
>


 -----  Posted via NewsOne.Net: Free (anonymous) Usenet News via the Web  -----
  http://newsone.net/ -- Free reading and anonymous posting to 60,000+ groups
   NewsOne.Net prohibits users from posting spam.  If this or other posts
made through NewsOne.Net violate posting guidelines, email abuse@newsone.net

<a id="t-457a6668c9"></a>
## Thread: Wanted: Humungous and Weird COBOL 01 COPYBOOKS — 1 msgs

_Topics: [`Jobs, careers, recruiting, salary`](topics.md#jobs)_

### Wanted: Humungous and Weird COBOL 01 COPYBOOKS

- **From:** Jim Keohane <jimkeo@lockstar.com>
- **Date:** 2001-05-07T16:57:49+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AF6D454.31AEED68@lockstar.com>`

```
Por favor,

    I'm playing with some code to parse and analyze COBOL 01 records
(WORKING-STORAGE, LINKAGE, FILE, whatever). I need to do some final
stress testing. I would prefer MVS or CICS COBOL but it needn't be
current. Old
VS/COBOL is fine. COBOL from other platforms is probably still
worthwhile
for stress testing.

    If you have any copybooks with a gazillion REDEFINEs and/or nesting
LEVELs about 50 deep and/or every COBOL data type and size imaginable or

that have OCCURS within OCCURS within OCCURS, etc. or that generate a
plethora of SLACK BYTES due to SYNC alignment or that need pad bytes in
GROUP levels with OCCURS > 1 then please email me a copy via
jimkeo@lockstar.com.

Thanks muchly in advance!  - Jim
```

toepassing hiervan heeft op de bedrijfsvoering van de klant. Dit betekent

<a id="t-0874ef5218"></a>
## Thread: replacing — 3 msgs

### replacing

- **From:** "Rolf" <rolf@kleinecke.net>
- **Date:** 2001-05-09T11:41:02+02:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9db34q$so3$00$1@news.t-online.com>`

```
hi together,

first, i am a (old) german programmer and my english is verry bad, sorry
therefore (not for the old german).
second, my question:
i have a copybook like this:

01 WKCRX-03.
    05 WKCRX-03-AMOUNT         PIC S9(15).
    05 WKCRX-03-SIGN                 PIC S9(01).
    .
    .
i will replace the prefix WKCRX by HKCRX.
I have coded :

COPY WKCRX-BOOK RELACING ==WKCRX== BY ==HKCRX==.

i know that i can not replace parts of not numeric literals. but i believe i
have used this method some years ago. but i am not sure.

my question is general: can i replace a prefix (a part of a field name) with
REPLACING??

thanks for answer

Rolf
```

#### ↳ Re: replacing

- **From:** "Ron" <Ron@NoSpamForMe.Com>
- **Date:** 2001-05-09T09:04:08-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<9dbiop$c93$1@sshuraac-i-1.production.compuserve.com>`
- **References:** `<9db34q$so3$00$1@news.t-online.com>`

```
You need to have delimiters, I used colons in the example below.
Make the copybook look like:
01 :WKCRX:-03.
     05 :WKCRX:-03-AMOUNT         PIC S9(15).
     05 :WKCRX:-03-SIGN                 PIC S9(01).

Then say:
COPY WKCRX-BOOK REPLACING ==:WKCRX:==  BY  ==HKCRX==.

Ron

===================================================

news:9db34q$so3$00$1@news.t-online.com...

with

>
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…

#### ↳ Re: (ACU)Cobol Data File dump

- **From:** "Michael Mattias" <michael.mattias@gte.net>
- **Date:** 2001-05-09T16:54:43+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<nDeK6.356$1k1.90344@dfiatx1-snr1.gtei.net>`
- **References:** `<hOaK6.120$pH4.4743@zonnet-reader-1>`

```
You can get a tutorial on COBOL datatypes by downloading file COBDATA.ZIP
from www.flexus.com/softwaredownload.html

As long as you have the FD, you will be in pretty good shape as long as you
are not looking at an indexed file with some kind of internal index
structure. Even then, I think ParseRat has something specifically for
ACUCOBOL files. (That's commercial software; don't know the price of a
license).
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: (ACU)Cobol Data File dump

- **From:** "Michael Mattias" <michael.mattias@gte.net>
- **Date:** 2001-05-09T18:28:13+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<1%fK6.401$1k1.113180@dfiatx1-snr1.gtei.net>`
- **References:** `<hOaK6.120$pH4.4743@zonnet-reader-1> <nDeK6.356$1k1.90344@dfiatx1-snr1.gtei.net> <IUeK6.136$pH4.5908@zonnet-reader-1>`

```
I don't know what you need to load the data into the MySQL DB, but to expect
you could find a "library" you could just 'plug in' is, well, 'ambitious.'
To think you could find such a library as freeware or PD software is just
plain wishful thinking.

I played with the ParseRat evauation package, too, and frankly I don't know
how you could find anything more straightforward than that; this kind of
data conversion is not an easy thing to do.

If you have an ACUCOBOL compiler - or know someone who does - the easiest
way to export this data is to write a program to extract data from the index
file and write it out into some "MySQL"-compatible format.

Meantime, read this:

http://www.flexus.com/ebd2asc.html

This deals with the whole issue of "I want to use COBOL-created data in my
non-COBOL application."


MCM
(author of said document)

- **From:** "Ray Smith" <Ray.Smith@fujitsu.com.au>

```
Hi,

If you have an AcuCobol file I would assume somewhere someone you are
working with has a registered
AcuCobol runtime.  If they have a runtime they should have access to a
program called "vutil" which will
unload any AcuCobol file into a plain text file.  Which you can then import
into anything.

If the file(s) are small you could send them to me, I'll unload them to text
files and send them back.

Ray Smith

#### ↳ Re: (ACU)Cobol Data File dump

- **From:** Vadim Maslov <vadik-nws@siber.com>
- **Date:** 2001-05-10T02:05:19+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3AF9F796.93454236@siber.com>`
- **References:** `<hOaK6.120$pH4.4743@zonnet-reader-1>`

```
There is one way...

Siber Systems has a library that can read AcuCobol data files.
Based on this library, we build a number of tools that can convert
Acu datafiles to CSV or DBF or even read them into Crystal Reports.

More on it here:
http://www.siber.com/sct/datafile/

The converter will cost you less than $500.
If you are only beginning to unedrstand the format
then the rest will take just 3 to 6 months.
So if you are not getting < $1/hour for your work,
our price should be relatively cheap.

Regards,
Vadim Maslov

## Thread: Announcing: TESTING COMPUTER SOFTWARE (TCS2001) Conference -- Keynote Speakers — 1 msgs

### Announcing: TESTING COMPUTER SOFTWARE (TCS2001) Conference -- Keynote Speakers

- **From:** pazpax@earthlink.net (TCS2001)
- **Date:** 2001-05-09T14:00:12+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.c++,comp.lang.cobol,comp.lang.forth
- **Message-ID:** `<pazpax-0905010900090001@1cust28.tnt37.tco2.da.uu.net>`

```

             18th International Conference and Exposition on 

                        TESTING COMPUTER SOFTWARE
              Theme: Meeting the New Challenges of Testing
                            June 18-22, 2001
            Washington, D.C. * Hyatt Regency (Bethesda) * USA 
        (Conference: June 20-22; Workshops/Tutorials: June 18-19)
  
In cooperation with:
Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) SIGSoft
American Society for Quality (ASQ) Software Division
IEEE Reliability Society
Software Technology Support Center (STSC)

                            * * * * * *

The general sessions of the Conference on Testing Computer Software will
address major issues of concern to the developers and users of software.
The theme of the Conference "Meeting the New Challenges of Testing"
focuses on providing practical and essential solutions to the software
development profession, and the users of computer software at all levels.

Tom Gilb's topic will be "Real Time Priority and Risk Management for World
Class Software Quality." He will discuss that world-class software quality
cannot be achieved using conventional thinking. Scarce human and time
resources must be managed in a dynamic way; with regard to multiple
stakeholder values and actual resource consumption. And multitudes of
potential threats to success must be identified and managed.

Warren S. Reid's will discuss "Is That Your Final Answer? Surviving Cross
Examination in the Courtroom." Lawsuits involving large-scale system
failures and dot.com failures are one of the fastest growing areas of
litigation in the legal profession today. Unexpectedly you may find
yourself in a courtroom with an unhappy vendor or customer. To stave off
being second-guessed, you will need to know how to perform, communicate,
and document your work in a way it will survive a courtroom test, and to
present your position with professional aplomb on the witness stand.

John Musa will cover "Efficient Testing with a Measure to Select Testing
Strategies." The problem of how to select the most efficient testing
strategies will be addressed. The market forces that are making it more
and more imperative to improve testing efficiency will be explored. The
requirements for selecting a measure to evaluate testing strategies and
tools will be summarized. A proposal for a measure is presented, along
with sufficient details of its application to show that most of the
potential problems have been addressed.

Edward Jones will present "Educators Join the Fray -- Tester Training for
Undergraduates" He will describe the efforts to expose undergraduate
students to the practice and principles of software testing. The goals of
this effort are three-fold: (1) give students hands-on experience in the
fundamentals of testing; (2) to transfer a quality-centric value system
(attitude towards testing/quality); (3) to transfer testing skills and
attitude into the undergraduate curriculum.

Simon Mills will explore "A World-Wide Web of Intrigue -- Deceptive
Testing." His talk covers the new issues related to e-commerce
applications, and the many old issues surfacing under a new guise. He will
present case study experiences to reflect "new challenges" as being
reflections of yesterdays problems.
.
The Conference will conclude with the wrap-up session: "Conference
Reflections - and a few surprises thrown in!" Marnie Hutcheson will
expertly summarize: What we did; Where we've been; Where we're going.

* Thirty-two Concurrent Sessions divided into four parallel tracks present
a wide variety of topics and issues in major areas of: Management,
Automation, Technology, Requirements, Practices, Web and Case Studies.

* Pre-Conference (June 18-19) Workshops (2 days) and Tutorials (1 day)
will present comprehensive technical sessions for software testing
practitioners. These sessions will provide detailed training on major
software testing technologies, tools and methodologies.

* Networking with participants interested in specialized topics will be
facilitated by Birds-of-a-Feather Sessions and Lunch Discussion Groups.

* The Conference Volume (including the presentations and proceedings of
the sessions) will be a valuable reference for testing projects.

* The Testing Tools vendors exhibit will present the latest in products
and services to support software and applications testing.

For a continuously updated program check out on the Web:
             <http://www.uspdi.org/conference/program.html>

                              * * * * *

For complete program details and registration please contact the U.S.
Professional Development Institute (USPDI):

Regular Mail: USPDI, 612 Ethan Allen Avenue, Suite 100; Takoma Park,
              MD 20912-5400, USA.
Phone:        [+1] 301-270-1033
Fax:          [+1] 301-270-1040
E-mail:       admin@uspdi.org (Subject: TCS2001)

On-line:
Brochure request: <http://www.uspdi.org/conference/brochure.html> 
Registration:     <http://www.uspdi.org/conference/register.html>
Web download:     <http://www.uspdi.org/conference/printbrochure.html>

                               * * * * *

                 ***
```

## Thread: Ugly IF Statement — 11 this month / 38 total

_Thread started [2001-04](2001-04.md#t-db5ea614b3)._

#### ↳ Re: Ugly IF Statement

- **From:** "Cliff Peterson" <cliffpeterson@spamcop.net>
- **Date:** 2001-05-10T02:44:02+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<SfnK6.13740$4f7.1067825@bgtnsc06-news.ops.worldnet.att.net>`
- **References:** `<20010410225436.26397.00004167@ng-cf1.aol.com>`

```
Eileen,

I can't resist a puzzle - I don't do crosswords, but love this stuff.

I'm just an old code pusher, but how about this?  Not shorter, but perhaps
clearer...

88s to group the cust-no and dest-cd could shorten and simplify as well.

EVALUATE TRUE
WHEN SPECIAL-CHRY-CLS
WHEN AIHD-CUST-NO   = 08221
WHEN AIHD-CUST-NO   = 05659
WHEN AIHD-CUST-NO   = 00879
WHEN AIHD-CUST-NO   = 08228
WHEN AIHD-CUST-NO   = 10418
WHEN AIHD-CUST-NO   = 11010
WHEN AIHD-CUST-NO   = 08288
WHEN AIHD-SALES-CLS = 11
WHEN AIHD-SALES-CLS = 34
WHEN AIHD-SALES-CLS = 35
WHEN AIHD-SALES-CLS = 61
    PERFORM XXXXXX

WHEN AIHD-SALES-CLS = 14
    EVALUATE WS-DEST-CD-1
    WHEN 'AP'
    WHEN 'WE'
    WHEN 'EE'
    WHEN 'EF'
    WHEN 'FD'
    WHEN 'PP'
    WHEN 'CC'
    WHEN 'IE'
        PERFORM XXXXXX
    END-EVALUATE

WHEN AIHD-SALES-CLS = 18
    EVALUATE WS-DEST-CD
    WHEN '48063'
    WHEN '17576'
        PERFORM XXXXXX
    END-EVALUATE

WHEN AIHD-SALES-CLS = 30
    EVALUATE WS-DEST-CD
    WHEN '04179'
    WHEN '12014'
    WHEN 'LSC07'
    WHEN '31   '
        PERFORM XXXXXX
    WHEN OTHER
        EVALUATE AIHD-CUST-NO
        WHEN 09937
        WHEN 10100
        WHEN 10181
        WHEN 10190
        WHEN 11100
        WHEN 09941
        WHEN 09949
        WHEN 10222
        WHEN 01552
        WHEN 11109
        WHEN 10108
            PERFORM xxxxxx
        END-EVALUATE
    END-EVALUATE

END-EVALUATE.



"YukonMama" <yukonmama@aol.com> wrote in message
news:20010410225436.26397.00004167@ng-cf1.aol.com...
> We get a lot of students either posting or lurking here so the other day
while
> working on a particularly nasty program (8000 some lines of spaghetti code
with
> polish logic) I came across this particular IF statement no less than 9
times
> in the program and thought they would like to see what's in store for
them.
>
> This is what you get to see in the real world :)
…[27 more quoted lines elided]…
> There are lower case x's at the PERFORM to indicate that different
paragraphs
> are performed each time the IF appeared in the program.  The paragraphs
were
> identical except for one or two variables from the map (this is a CICS
> program).  These variables are actually an occurance but the map is not
> redefined so each time the field in question is used such as QTY1 or QTY2
the
> paragraph is duplicated.  Makes maintenance a real nightmare.
>
> You will also notice that there are a few ORs that preclude the execution
of
> other ORs (that is it doesn't matter as something before it takes
precedent -
> assignment: see if you can find it).
>
> The fields being tested are in the header record which is read at the
beginning
> of the program.  The IF statement is now reworked and a switch set if
> everything makes it thru the tangle and the switch checked where each of
the
> original IFs were.  Just my part of making this nightmare a bit more
managable.
>
> Eileen
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
>

- **Message-ID:** `<ezUL6.22224$4f7.1763596@bgtnsc06-news.ops.worldnet.att.net>`
- **References:** `<20010410225436.26397.00004167@ng-cf1.aol.com> <SfnK6.13740$4f7.1067825@bgtnsc06-news.ops.worldnet.att.net> <0NvK6.228$uV4.28222@dfiatx1-snr1.gtei.net> <3afb3b49_2@Usenet.com>`

"Peter E. C. Dashwood" <dashwood@free.net.nz> wrote in message
news:3afb3b49_2@Usenet.com...
> Michael Mattias wrote in message
> > Much clearer! Very nice!
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> Pete.

Pete,

As the "original poster", I have a few comments.

I'm a day-to-day coder, not the Tintoretto of  Cobol.

If I ran across the code originally posted during a maintenance project, I
would NOT...
- rewrite the program,
- restructure the file logic,
- take the previous programmers to task,
- attempt to dazzle the shop with my scintillating intellect.

If I've been asked to change the program, my job is to do just that - not
prove my adherence to the latest Cobol religion.

I WOULD...
- decompose an IF statement into a more readable form if I was having
trouble understanding it (you see the result),
- replace it in the program only if I felt sure I could test the resultant
code within my allocation of hours,
- print it and pin it to my cube wall for next time if I couldn't.

Now...

I submit that my code, in my humble opinion, is easier to read and
understand than the originally posted version and does not intrude on other
functioning code.  My purpose in submitting it was within this context, not
to be sniffed at by Cobol "instructors" for its lack of, well, whatever.

Assuming that the MIS in this shop hasn't personally asked you to beautify
this code, what is your solution, Pete?


Cliff
```
